data.title: Mittal’s Rajasthan Royals Deal Sparks UK Shareholder Feud
data.description:
Billionaire Lakshmi Mittal’s plan to acquire the Rajasthan Royals cricket franchise has triggered a London lawsuit, with minority shareholders alleging they are being forced to surrender shares worth about $50 million for just £1 ($1.33), according to Bloomberg.

The dispute centers on the company that owns the team.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Amazon Integrates Alexa+ With OpenTable, Grubhub, Uber Eats, Amazon Fresh, and Whole Foods
data.description: According to Jin10, Amazon said Alexa+ integrates with OpenTable, Grubhub, Uber Eats, Amazon Fresh, and Whole Foods to enable voice commerce.

data.title: Market Tends | Meituan Tests Traffic-Light Pause Feature for Couriers
data.description: Meituan has initially built a traffic-light pause feature, and it is now in internal testing. According to Jin10, once the feature goes live, the system will time deliveries when couriers encounter a red light, and after data is improved this year, it is expected to be rolled out gradually to cover more than 1 million couriers.

data.title: South East Water Secures £200 Million Liquidity Boost
data.description: South East Water Ltd. struck an agreement for £200 million ($266 million) of new liquidity, which will help the UK utility boost its cash coffers, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Iran, Pakistan Foreign Ministers Meet on SCO Sidelines
data.description: According to Jin10, Tasnim News Agency said Iran and Pakistan's foreign ministers met on the sidelines of a Shanghai Cooperation Organization meeting to discuss bilateral issues.

data.title: Iranian Health Ministry Spokesperson: Death Toll From Recent U.S. Strikes on Iran Rises to 55
data.description: Iranian Health Ministry spokesperson said the death toll from recent U.S. military strikes on Iran has risen to 55, and the number of injured has climbed to 645. According to Jin10, the spokesperson gave the latest figures.

data.title: NVIDIA Sends Price Increase Notice to Board Partners for GDDR6 and GDDR7 Memory Kits
data.description: NVIDIA (NVDA.O) has sent a price increase notice to its board partners for GDDR6 and GDDR7 memory kits. According to Odaily, NVIDIA supplies GPU chips and VRAM chips to partners as kits, which the partners install on their own PCB designs to produce custom graphics cards. With another price increase expected for the GDDR6 and GDDR7 memory kits, AIB makers are expected to raise graphics card prices accordingly.

data.title: Iranian and Pakistani Foreign Ministers Discuss Recent U.S. Airstrikes
data.description: According to Jin10, market sources said Iran's foreign minister and Pakistan's foreign minister discussed recent U.S. airstrikes.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 65,000 USDT with a 1.14% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jul 24, 2026, 09:22 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 65,000 USDT and is now trading at 64,982.011719 USDT, with a narrowed 1.14% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: STOCKS | BOE Varitronix Purchases 1 Million H Shares for Share Award Plan
data.description: BOE Varitronix announced that its trustee bought a total of 1,000,000 shares from the market between July 20, 2026, and July 24, 2026, for the share award plan under the terms and conditions of the share award plan and trust deed. According to Jin10, the average consideration per share was HK$3.9776, and the total cost was HK$3.9776 million, excluding transaction expenses.

data.title: Shanghai Baoshan State-Owned Capital Fund Launches With 500 Million Yuan Capital
data.description: Shanghai Baoshan Guochuang Private Fund Partnership (limited partnership) was established on July 23 with registered capital of 500 million yuan, according to Jiemian News. The fund's executive partner is Shanghai Baoshan Guotou Private Fund Management Co., Ltd., and its business scope covers private-fund equity investment, investment management and asset management. Partnership records show it is jointly funded by Shanghai Baoshan State-Owned Capital Investment Management Group Co., Ltd., Shanghai Baoheng Economic Development Co., Ltd. and Shanghai Baoshan Guotou Private Fund Management Co., Ltd.

data.title: 30-Year Treasury Bond ETF Boshi Sees 153 Million Yuan Net Inflow
data.description: On July 24, 2026, the 30-Year Treasury Bond ETF Boshi (511130.SH) closed up 0.09% and recorded a net inflow of 153 million yuan in large orders, according to Jiemian News. Over the past three trading days, the fund saw cumulative large-order inflows of 687 million yuan. Its latest trading volume was 20.982 million shares, and turnover reached 2.245 billion yuan.

data.title: Secret Service Says Threats Against Protected People Are Up About 40%
data.description: According to Axios, the U.S. Secret Service says threats against people it protects are up about 40% from a year ago, with roughly 10,000 threat cases investigated so far this year. The agency said mental health interventions have risen tenfold from the same period last year, including referrals and both voluntary and involuntary commitments involving people encountered during its protective mission. Secret Service spokesperson Anthony Guglielmi said the increase is forcing the agency to devote more agents and investigative resources to protecting senior officials, while Director Sean Curran said the agency expects bad actors to show up but is treating Friday's rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner like any other presidential site. The Secret Service protects the president, Supreme Court justices, cabinet members, former presidents, presidential candidates and visiting foreign leaders.

data.title: U.S. Uses B-1 Bomber To Strike Iran Targets As Conflict Escalates
data.description: According to Axios, the U.S. military used a B-1 long-range bomber on Tuesday to strike Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps targets in Iran, marking the first B-1 mission since fighting with Iran resumed 12 days ago. U.S. officials said the bomber launched from an airbase in the U.K., and U.S. Central Command said its assets targeted Iranian military operations centers, maritime capabilities, aircraft hangars, drone storage facilities and military logistics infrastructure to try to further degrade Iran's ability to threaten commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The report said it was unclear what the B-1 struck and whether the mission was more effective than earlier attacks. It also said President Trump is still considering a return to major combat operations against Iran, with U.S. and Israeli officials saying that could happen within days.

data.title: STOCKS | Yue Yuen Issues Profit Warning For First-Half 2026
data.description:
Yue Yuen Industrial (0551) said it expects first-half 2026 net profit to fall by about 55% to 60% year on year, according to Ming Pao. The company said first-half 2025 net profit was about $171 million.

Yue Yuen said the decline mainly reflects multiple challenges in its manufacturing business, including weak demand that reduced sales volume, along with rising costs and pressure on production efficiency. The company said these factors hurt gross margin in its manufacturing segment during the period.

data.title: South Korean Court Reduces Crypto Executive Fraud Sentence to Six Years and Six Months
data.description:
A South Korean appeals court reduced a crypto executive’s fraud sentence to six years and six months after prosecutors accused the executive of deceiving more than 30 investors. According to NS3.AI, the case involved alleged losses of over 3 billion won.

The coin promoted to investors was never listed domestically.

data.title: Samsung Says Samsung Wallet Will Support Native Stablecoins
data.description: Samsung said at the 2026 Galaxy Unpacked event that Samsung Wallet will support native stablecoins. According to NS3.AI, a demo screen showed Circle’s USDC, but Samsung did not name USDC or USDT as partners. Samsung also unveiled Samsung Galaxy Card with 5% cash back on eligible spending.

data.title: UK Food Prices Seen Peaking Lower Than Thought, BOE Survey Finds
data.description: Britain’s supermarkets expect to raise food prices by less than previously thought as higher fuel costs tied to the Middle East conflict weaken household demand, according to Bloomberg. The Bank of England said in a survey of regional business contacts that grocers are planning smaller price increases than earlier expected.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Indian Court: OpenAI Using ANI Content for AI Training Does Not Constitute Copyright Infringement
data.description: An Indian court ruled that OpenAI's use of news agency ANI's content for AI training does not constitute copyright infringement. According to Jin10, that is the only detail provided.

data.title: Oil Prices Give Back Gains After One-Day Surge, Still Up Double Digits This Week
data.description: According to Jin10, oil prices gave back gains after a one-day surge, but reduced output in the dual waterways and the Black Sea still supported this week's double-digit rise.
